questions.questionText: Which pillar of OOP allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method defined in its superclass?
questions.correct: Polymorphism (Method Overriding)
wrong: Encapsulation
Inheritance (Method Overloading)
Abstraction execution rules

questions.questionText: How do you enforce strict Encapsulation inside a Java POJO bean class definition?
questions.correct: Declare fields private and expose access points strictly via public getter and setter methods
wrong: Mark the class structure final and avoid using constructors
Turn all method trackers static
Route all object initializations through separate database loaders

questions.questionText: What does a class inherit when it implements a Java Interface containing default methods?
questions.correct: The interface's default functional implementations without breaking backward compatibility
wrong: The concrete private instance fields
Complete access to the abstract module's private memory
Automated thread synchronization parameters across loop executions

questions.questionText: What restriction applies to a class declared with the final modifier keyword in Java?
questions.correct: The class cannot be extended or subclassed by any other class structure
wrong: The internal variables are rendered immutable values instantly
The class cannot contain any public instance initialization constructors
The JVM forces code tasks to run inside kernel mode

questions.questionText: What separates method overriding from method overloading in Java programming execution?
questions.correct: Overriding replaces a superclass method at runtime; overloading uses distinct parameters at compile-time
wrong: Overloading is exclusive to abstract interfaces
Overriding modifies class access modifiers; overloading alters return type bounds
Overloading forces garbage collection routines

questions.questionText: What structural trait differentiates a Checked Exception from an Unchecked Exception in Java?
questions.correct: Checked exceptions are verified at compile-time and must be caught or declared in the method signature
wrong: Unchecked exceptions cause physical RAM allocation failures
Checked exceptions can only be thrown from inside static class routines
Unchecked exceptions are skipped entirely by the JVM debugger

questions.questionText: How does the try-with-resources statement handle asset hygiene automatically on block exits?
questions.correct: It automatically invokes .close() on any resource object that implements AutoCloseable
wrong: It flushes the CPU cache logs to clear remaining variables
It dispatches a dedicated Garbage Collection sweep task
It saves an execution checkpoint status state directly back to tables

questions.questionText: What block in a try-catch-finally layout is guaranteed to execute regardless of whether an exception is thrown?
questions.correct: The finally block
wrong: The catch block
The try block
The throws block

questions.questionText: What type of exception is a NullPointerException classified as inside the Java runtime architecture?
questions.correct: An Unchecked Exception (RuntimeException)
wrong: A Checked Exception
An Error exception condition
A Compile-time syntax flag anomaly

questions.questionText: What happens if a catch block intercepts an exception and rethrows a new RuntimeException wrapper?
questions.correct: The current execution path breaks and the exception propagates up the thread stack frame
wrong: The JVM defaults all text inputs back to empty strings
The active relational database transaction commits its row status
The operating system kernel terminates the parent thread launcher instance

questions.questionText: What is a major performance difference between an ArrayList and a LinkedList in Java?
questions.correct: ArrayList provides O(1) random access time; LinkedList provides O(1) element insertion at boundaries
wrong: LinkedList consumes less memory because it drops link node pointer sets
ArrayList resizes dynamically without duplicating its internal index arrays
LinkedList supports horizontal clustering distributions

questions.questionText: Which interface inside the Java Collections Framework guarantees that no duplicate elements can be stored?
questions.correct: Set
wrong: List
Queue
Map entries tracker

questions.questionText: How does a HashMap handle key value lookups efficiently under the hood?
questions.correct: It computes the key's hashCode() to locate a bucket array slot containing data node links
wrong: It runs a full linear search loop across all entries sequentially
It references a foreign key constraint row mapped from a local tracker
It writes the object properties out to temporary text files

questions.questionText: What behavioral trait separates a TreeMap from a standard HashMap implementation?
questions.correct: TreeMap maintains its keys sorted according to their natural ordering or a custom Comparator
wrong: HashMap encrypts key fields; TreeMap leaves characters visible
TreeMap is limited to storing integer primitive values
TreeMap prevents asynchronous thread execution steps from accessing data

questions.questionText: What performance penalty can hit a HashMap if multiple distinct keys yield identical hashCode values?
questions.correct: Hash collisions increase, causing lookup speeds to degrade from O(1) toward O(N)
wrong: The JVM throws a ConcurrentModificationException and aborts the thread
The internal memory mapping flushes all cached key allocations
The database loader class drops its connection stream pipe instantly

questions.questionText: What primary objective does Java's Type Erasure accomplish when compiling generic code architectures?
questions.correct: It replaces type parameters with bounds or Object to ensure type safety without runtime overhead
wrong: It forces the compiler to duplicate class files for every unique type
It converts object reference addresses into encrypted byte arrays
It restricts collection instances from scaling horizontally

questions.questionText: What does the wildcard bound pattern 'List<? extends Number>' accomplish inside a method signature?
questions.correct: It restricts the parameter to a List containing Number or any of its subclasses
wrong: It opens the List to accept additions of any custom object structure
It mandates that all elements in the List must carry matching static values
It turns the List into an immutable collection

questions.questionText: Why are primitive types like int or char forbidden from being passed directly as parameters to Java Generics?
questions.correct: Generics require object references on the heap; primitives do not inherit from Object
wrong: Primitives consume too much L1 cache memory causing thread scheduler stalls
The modularity guidelines written in module-info block primitive parameters
Database drivers cannot bind primitive types to statement query parameters

questions.questionText: What happens when you use a raw type instead of a parameterized generic type declaration?
questions.correct: You lose compile-time type safety checking benefits and trigger compiler warning logs
wrong: The application crashes instantly with a ClassCastException upon boot paths
The Java Virtual Machine executes the bytecode lines 50 percent slower
Jackson's ObjectMapper becomes unable to parse JSON resource file streams

questions.questionText: What does the wildcard pattern 'List<? super Integer>' signify inside type boundaries checking?
questions.correct: It specifies a List that can hold Integer or any of its master superclass ancestor layers
wrong: It limits the collection strictly to elements that implement serializable
It prevents downstream functions from executing list mutations
It tells the system that the collection properties are bound to an index

questions.questionText: What thread safety violation occurs when a thread caches local variable copies instead of reading from main RAM?
questions.correct: Memory Visibility Problem
wrong: Race Condition anomaly
Deadlock layout lock
Illegal Thread State condition

questions.questionText: What modifier keyword forces Java threads to read and write variable values directly from main memory?
questions.correct: volatile
wrong: synchronized
static
transient properties tracking

questions.questionText: What occurs if an execution thread invokes .wait() on an object without holding that object's monitor lock?
questions.correct: It throws an immediate IllegalMonitorStateException at runtime
wrong: The thread drops into a safe background daemon sleep loop
The underlying database statement execution rolls back its current update
The context switch routine allocates more processing cycles

questions.questionText: Which thread-safe collection from java.util.concurrent should replace a standard HashMap in high-concurrency environments?
questions.correct: ConcurrentHashMap
wrong: Collections.synchronizedMap
Hashtable
LinkedHashMap module structure

questions.questionText: What structural capability advantage does a Callable task possess compared to a standard Runnable task?
questions.correct: A Callable can return a computed result value and throw checked exceptions
wrong: A Callable runs directly inside kernel privilege rings bypassing restrictions
A Callable can be restarted multiple times within the same thread lifecycle
A Callable does not require module-info reflection opens configurations
